views.definition:
SELECT duty_log_id,
    crew_id,
    flight_id,
    duty_start,
    duty_end
   FROM dutylog;

entity_relationships.related_entities: Training, Flight, Leave, Expense, Compliance, Notification, ReferenceData
entity_relationships.business_meaning: Crew is the central entity; one crew member can have many training enrollments (1:N), many flight assignments (1:N), many leave requests (1:N), many expense claims (1:N), many compliance violations (1:N), and many notifications (1:N). Each crew member belongs to one rank and one base (N:1).
Training programs contain many training sessions (1:N), and each session can have many crew enrollments (1:N). Simulator sessions link crew to specific aircraft types (N:1).
Flights are assigned to specific aircraft types (N:1) and have many crew assignments (1:N). Roster periods group multiple flight assignments (1:N). Duty logs record per-flight crew activity (1:N).
Leave requests are submitted by crew members (N:1) against a leave type (N:1). Availability and rest period records track crew readiness windows (N:1 to crew).
Expense claims are submitted by crew members (N:1) against an expense type (N:1) for reimbursement processing.
Compliance violations are recorded against individual crew members (N:1), often triggered by expired licenses, insufficient rest, or training gaps.
Notifications are delivered to specific crew members (N:1) for events such as roster changes, training reminders, and compliance alerts.
Reference tables (crewrank, crewbase, license, leavetype, expensetype) provide lookup values used across all operational entities (1:N relationships).

use_cases.name: Crew Rostering
use_cases.description: Assign qualified and rested crew members to upcoming flights, ensuring each flight has the required roles and that crew are within regulatory duty limits.
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, crewassignment, flight, roster, restperiod, availability, crewaircraftrating

use_cases.name: Training Compliance Tracking
use_cases.description: Monitor crew training enrollment, completion, and simulator sessions to ensure all required certifications are current before flight assignment.
use_cases.involved_tables: trainingprogram, trainingsession, trainingenrollment, simulatorsession, crew, complianceviolation

use_cases.name: Leave and Availability Management
use_cases.description: Process crew leave requests, manage availability windows, and ensure adequate coverage during planned absences.

use_cases.name: Expense Reimbursement
use_cases.description: Track and approve crew expense claims categorized by expense type for payroll and reimbursement processing.

use_cases.name: Regulatory Compliance Monitoring
use_cases.description: Detect crew members with expiring medical certificates, licenses, or insufficient rest periods to prevent compliance violations.
use_cases.involved_tables: medicalcertificate, crewlicense, license, restperiod, complianceviolation, auditlog, notification

sample_queries.question: Find all crew members who are currently available and qualified for a specific aircraft type
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, cr.rank_name, cb.base_name
FROM crew c
JOIN crewrank cr ON c.rank_id = cr.rank_id
JOIN crewbase cb ON c.base_id = cb.base_id
JOIN crewaircraftrating car ON c.crew_id = car.crew_id
JOIN availability a ON c.crew_id = a.crew_id
WHERE car.aircraft_type_id = :aircraft_type_id
  AND a.is_available = TRUE
  AND a.start_date <= CURRENT_DATE
  AND a.end_date >= CURRENT_DATE
  AND c.crew_id NOT IN (
    SELECT crew_id FROM leaverequest
    WHERE status = 'Approved' AND start_date <= CURRENT_DATE AND end_date >= CURRENT_DATE
  );

sample_queries.question: Which crew members have medical certificates expiring within the next 30 days?
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, mc.expiry_date
FROM crew c
JOIN medicalcertificate mc ON c.crew_id = mc.crew_id
WHERE mc.expiry_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE AND CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '30 days'
ORDER BY mc.expiry_date;

sample_queries.question: Total flight duty hours logged per crew member in the current month
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name,
       SUM(dl.duty_hours) AS total_duty_hours,
       COUNT(DISTINCT dl.flight_id) AS flights_worked
FROM crew c
JOIN dutylog dl ON c.crew_id = dl.crew_id
WHERE dl.log_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_duty_hours DESC;

sample_queries.question: List all compliance violations in the last 90 days with associated crew details
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT cv.violation_id, cv.violation_type, cv.violation_date, cv.severity,
       c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, cr.rank_name
FROM complianceviolation cv
JOIN crew c ON cv.crew_id = c.crew_id
JOIN crewrank cr ON c.rank_id = cr.rank_id
WHERE cv.violation_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days'
ORDER BY cv.violation_date DESC;

sample_queries.question: Which crew members are assigned to a specific flight with their roles and rest compliance?
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, cr.rank_name,
       ca.role, f.flight_number, f.departure_date,
       rp.rest_hours
FROM crewassignment ca
JOIN crew c ON ca.crew_id = c.crew_id
JOIN crewrank cr ON c.rank_id = cr.rank_id
JOIN flight f ON ca.flight_id = f.flight_id
LEFT JOIN restperiod rp ON c.crew_id = rp.crew_id
  AND rp.end_date = f.departure_date
WHERE f.flight_id = :flight_id
  AND (rp.rest_hours >= 10 OR rp.rest_hours IS NULL);
